title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent TypeScript team receives different tenants, browsers, file sizes, media, regions, compliance, traffic, and cost constraints and transfers edge security, waf, headers, and origin containment into a new layered edge-security plan. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of layered edge-security plan with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Waf Rate Bot Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2509 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to exhaustively evaluate application, WAF, origin, header, and private-error controls; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then change region, retrieval, or transfer cost and defend the revised budget action. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Waf Rate Bot Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for in a strict node 24 and typescript 7 service, apply waf, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence. For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Waf Rate Bot Boundary, keep Waf Rate Bot Boundary connected to executable TypeScript storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A lab TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 2509 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.instruction:
